wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MedVita healthcare management platform overview. Explain its purpose as a modern healthcare solution, key features including role-based access for doctors and patients, appointment management, prescription creation, and medical history tracking. Document the target audience, core benefits, and how it streamlines clinical workflows. Include the technology stack overview covering React, Vite, Supabase, Tailwind CSS, and other key dependencies. Provide context about the healthcare industry challenges it addresses and the value proposition for medical professionals and patients. Use terminology consistent with the healthcare and technology domains.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for MedVita's React application. Describe the component hierarchy starting from main.jsx entry point through App.jsx router configuration to individual page components. Document the context provider pattern implementation for authentication and theme management, explaining how AuthContext.jsx and ThemeContext.jsx manage global state across components. Detail the routing strategy using React Router DOM, including protected routes and navigation patterns. Explain the component composition approach, reusable UI components, and styling strategy with Tailwind CSS. Document the integration with Supabase client library for database operations and authentication. Include architectural patterns like higher-order components, custom hooks, and state management strategies. Address performance considerations, code splitting opportunities, and bundle optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user roles and permissions in MedVita. Document the three user roles: Doctor, Patient, and Receptionist, including their specific permissions, access levels, and capabilities. Explain the role-based access control implementation in the database schema and how roles are enforced through Supabase Row Level Security policies. Detail the profile creation process during user registration and how role metadata is stored and managed. Include practical examples of role checking in components, permission-based UI rendering, and role-specific feature access. Address role transitions, permission inheritance, and security implications of each role type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for MedVita's database schema. Detail all table relationships including profiles, patients, appointments, prescriptions, and doctor_availability with their foreign key constraints and referential integrity rules. Document the primary key relationships, cascading actions, and join patterns used throughout the application. Include entity relationship diagrams showing cardinality and participation constraints. Explain how the multi-role system (doctor, patient, receptionist) affects data relationships and access patterns. Cover the patient-doctor relationship structure, receptionist-employer relationship, and appointment-patient linkage mechanisms. Provide practical examples of common JOIN operations and query patterns used in the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Hierarchy & Composition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component hierarchy documentation for MedVita's React application. Detail the component tree structure starting from App.jsx as the root component, explaining how Layout.jsx orchestrates the overall page structure with Header and Sidebar components. Document component composition patterns, prop drilling prevention strategies, and component reusability principles. Explain the relationship between page-level components and shared UI components. Include examples of component composition patterns used throughout the application, such as the protected route pattern and dashboard layouts. Address component lifecycle management, performance optimization through proper component structuring, and best practices for maintaining clean component hierarchies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Doctor Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Doctor Dashboard functionality in MedVita. Detail the live queue management system including real-time patient tracking, queue status updates, and patient vitals display. Document the Today's Queue Panel with its interactive features for marking patients as seen, creating prescriptions, and managing patient flow. Explain the patient engagement chart for visualizing patient visit patterns and trends. Cover the quick actions panel for adding patients, creating appointments, and generating prescriptions. Include the statistics cards showing total patients, appointments, and earnings. Document the integration with Supabase realtime channels for live updates, the prescription creation workflow, and the doctor-specific navigation patterns. Address performance considerations for real-time queue updates, accessibility features for medical professionals, and customization options for dashboard widgets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Registration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for patient registration functionality in MedVita. Detail the patient onboarding process including form validation, data collection, and profile creation workflows. Document the registration form fields (name, age, sex, contact information, vitals), input validation rules, and error handling mechanisms. Explain the integration with doctor profiles and how patient-doctor relationships are established. Cover the unique patient ID generation system, automatic timestamping, and data sanitization processes. Include examples of successful registration flows, common validation errors, and troubleshooting steps for registration failures. Address security considerations for sensitive health information and compliance with healthcare data protection standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Booking Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the appointment booking interface component. Document the modal-based booking system that allows patients and doctors to schedule appointments through a user-friendly form. Explain the form validation, entity selection (doctor/patient dropdowns), date picker functionality, and time slot selection grid. Cover the responsive design implementation with mobile-first considerations, including the three-view calendar interface (month, week, list modes). Detail the integration with Supabase for real-time data synchronization and the Google Calendar sync functionality. Include examples of booking workflows, error handling scenarios, and the fallback mechanisms for calendar synchronization failures. Address the different user roles and their specific booking capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase Realtime Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Supabase Realtime integration in MedVita. Detail the implementation of PostgreSQL Realtime channels for live data synchronization, including channel creation, event filtering, and subscription management. Explain the doctor queue channel setup with doctor-specific filters, event handling for INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations, and real-time UI updates. Document the stable ref pattern for maintaining latest state setters in realtime callbacks, connection lifecycle management, and proper cleanup procedures. Include examples of custom event filtering, channel naming conventions, and performance optimization techniques for real-time subscriptions. Address error handling, reconnection strategies, and debugging realtime connections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prescription Creation Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the prescription creation interface component. Document the user interface design including diagnosis input, treatment/rx text area, patient email field, and form validation. Explain the component's state management for diagnosis and prescription text, auto-filling mechanisms for patient emails, and edit mode functionality. Cover the form submission workflow including data preparation, payload construction, and integration with Supabase database operations. Detail the real-time status indicators showing saving, generating, uploading, and emailing phases. Include examples of prescription text formatting, diagnosis entry patterns, and error handling during form submission. Address accessibility features, responsive design considerations, and user experience optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Provider Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ThemeProvider implementation in MedVita. Detail the theme state management including light/dark mode detection, localStorage persistence, and system preference integration. Explain the context provider pattern implementation, theme switching mechanisms, and state synchronization across components. Document the useEffect hook for applying theme classes to the document root element, including CSS class management and attribute setting. Cover the useTheme custom hook implementation, error handling for context usage, and theme state initialization from multiple sources (localStorage, system preferences). Include examples of theme state updates, persistence mechanisms, and integration patterns with other components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Google Calendar Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Google Calendar integration documentation for MedVita's appointment synchronization system. Detail the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow using Google Identity Services, including client ID configuration, API key setup, and scope management. Document the calendar event creation process, including date/time formatting, timezone handling, and reminder configuration. Explain the dual authentication approach supporting both OAuth and direct URL generation methods. Cover the implementation of calendar event synchronization, error handling strategies, and token management. Include setup instructions for Google Cloud Console, API enablement, and environment variable configuration. Address common integration issues such as authentication failures, quota limits, and permission errors. Provide troubleshooting guides for calendar sync problems and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase REST API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive REST API documentation for Supabase database operations in MedVita. Document all CRUD endpoints for core entities including profiles, patients, appointments, prescriptions, and doctor_availability tables. Detail table schemas, column definitions, and data types. Explain query patterns including SELECT statements with WHERE clauses, INSERT operations with RETURNING clauses, UPDATE commands with WHERE conditions, and DELETE operations. Document authentication methods using JWT tokens and session management. Include request/response schemas, parameter validation rules, and error handling strategies. Provide practical examples of common operations like patient registration, appointment booking, and prescription retrieval. Address pagination, filtering, sorting, and search capabilities. Document row level security policies and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design System Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive design system documentation for MedVita's UI components. Detail the foundational design principles including color palette (teal, blue, red, gray schemes), typography hierarchy, spacing system, and animation guidelines. Document the design tokens used throughout the component library, including semantic color meanings, sizing scales, and elevation levels. Explain the design philosophy behind component variations and how they align with healthcare UI best practices. Include guidelines for maintaining design consistency, accessibility standards, and responsive design patterns. Provide examples of proper design system usage and common anti-patterns to avoid.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing implementation in MedVita. Document the Vitest testing framework setup, Jest DOM configuration, and testing utilities. Explain component testing patterns for React functional components, custom hooks testing, and utility function testing. Cover mocking strategies for Supabase client, authentication contexts, and external APIs. Include examples of testing React components with React Testing Library, testing async operations, and handling component lifecycle. Document testing best practices for healthcare-specific components, form validation, and state management. Address testing of UI components, input validation, and error boundary scenarios. Provide practical examples of writing effective unit tests and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed build configuration documentation for MedVita's frontend application. Document the Vite build system configuration including development and production settings, asset optimization, and bundle analysis. Explain the package.json scripts for building, serving, and testing the application. Detail the HTML template configuration, public asset management, and static file handling. Cover build optimization techniques including code splitting, tree shaking, and minification. Include environment variable handling during build time, asset fingerprinting for cache busting, and build performance monitoring. Provide troubleshooting guides for common build issues, memory optimization, and build pipeline debugging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Supabase deployment documentation covering database provisioning, edge function deployment, and real-time subscription setup. Document the Supabase CLI deployment process including project initialization, database schema migration, and edge function compilation. Explain environment variable configuration for Supabase projects, including API keys, database URLs, and edge function secrets. Detail the edge function deployment workflow for send-prescription-email and other serverless functions. Cover database schema management, row level security policy deployment, and table relationship setup. Include deployment verification procedures, health checks for database connectivity, and function execution monitoring. Address Supabase dashboard configuration, custom domain setup, and SSL certificate management. Document backup strategies, database optimization, and performance monitoring for production workloads.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for MedVita. Include prerequisites covering Node.js, Git, and Supabase account setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions from cloning the repository to running the development server. Document environment variable configuration with detailed examples for Supabase credentials. Explain the project structure and key directories. Include initial setup verification steps and common troubleshooting for setup issues. Provide guidance for different development environments and deployment targets. Make content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for MedVita's Supabase-based backend system. Describe the database-first approach with PostgreSQL schema design, including table relationships, foreign key constraints, and Row Level Security policies. Document the edge functions architecture using TypeScript for serverless computing, focusing on the send-prescription-email function as a case study. Explain the Supabase configuration management through config.toml, including authentication settings, storage configuration, and function deployment. Detail the separation between client-side logic and server-side business logic, highlighting security implications and data access patterns. Document the real-time capabilities through Supabase Realtime channels and their integration with frontend components. Address scalability considerations, database optimization strategies, and backup/recovery procedures. Include deployment architecture showing the relationship between Supabase infrastructure and external service integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication flow in MedVita. Detail the complete user journey from registration through login to session establishment. Document the sign-up process including role selection, profile creation, and automatic profile table population via database triggers. Explain the login flow with credential validation, session creation, and user state management. Cover the Supabase auth state change listeners, automatic session restoration on app load, and real-time authentication state updates. Include error handling for authentication failures, password reset procedures, and account verification processes. Provide code examples of authentication methods and state management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Row Level Security Policies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MedVita's Row Level Security (RLS) implementation. Explain the security policies applied to each table including profiles, patients, doctor_availability, appointments, and prescriptions. Document the policy conditions, user role-based access controls, and data isolation mechanisms. Detail how the policies ensure proper access control for different user roles (doctor, patient, receptionist). Include examples of policy expressions, authentication checks, and authorization logic. Explain the security implications of each policy and how they prevent unauthorized data access. Cover the auto-generated profile creation trigger and its security considerations. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common RLS policy conflicts and access denied scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management & Context API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed state management documentation covering MedVita's React Context API implementation. Explain the authentication state management through AuthContext.jsx, including user session handling, role-based access control, and login/logout flows. Document the theme state management via ThemeContext.jsx for dark/light mode switching. Detail the ProtectedRoute implementation for enforcing role-based access control across different dashboard routes. Include practical examples of context consumption in components, state update patterns, and performance considerations with context providers. Address state persistence strategies, context provider composition, and best practices for avoiding unnecessary re-renders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Patient Dashboard functionality in MedVita. Document the upcoming visits section showing today's scheduled appointments with doctor names and timing. Explain the statistics cards displaying total prescriptions and appointment history. Cover the quick actions for booking new appointments and viewing prescription history. Detail the patient-specific data visualization showing appointment trends and engagement patterns. Include the navigation flow from dashboard to appointment booking and prescription viewing. Document the responsive design adaptations for mobile and desktop views. Address the patient's perspective on accessing their medical information, appointment scheduling convenience, and prescription management. Explain the integration with appointment and prescription data sources, and how the dashboard maintains patient privacy and security standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for patient profile management capabilities in MedVita. Document the CRUD operations for patient records including viewing, editing, updating, and deleting patient information. Explain the patient details modal functionality, vitals tracking (blood pressure, heart rate), and profile photo handling. Cover the integration with real-time data updates, search and filtering mechanisms, and bulk operations. Detail the patient status indicators, active/inactive flags, and prescription history display. Include examples of profile modification workflows, data validation during edits, and audit trail considerations. Address emergency contact management, medical history integration, and profile synchronization across different user roles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Availability Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the doctor availability management system. Detail the interface for configuring working hours, break schedules, and special appointment types. Explain the time slot configuration, capacity limits per time slot, and conflict detection mechanisms. Document the availability CRUD operations, bulk updates, and recurring schedule patterns. Cover the integration with the booking system to prevent double-booking and manage appointment capacity. Include examples of setting up daily schedules, handling overlapping appointments, and managing doctor availability changes. Address the relationship between availability settings and the calendar views in the booking interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Queue Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the live queue management system in MedVita's doctor dashboard. Explain the queue panel implementation with real-time patient tracking, status updates, and visual indicators. Document the patient positioning algorithm, queue sorting logic, and real-time status synchronization between doctors and receptionists. Cover the animation system using Framer Motion for smooth queue transitions, the mark-as-seen functionality with optimistic updates, and the integration with vitals monitoring. Include examples of queue state management, real-time filtering, and performance considerations for large patient volumes. Address edge cases like concurrent updates, offline scenarios, and data consistency guarantees.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PDF generation system used in prescription creation. Detail the html2canvas integration for rendering prescription templates to canvas, including scaling factors, image quality settings, and A4 dimension specifications. Explain the jsPDF conversion process, image compression techniques, and blob generation for cloud storage. Document the hidden mirror rendering technique used for accurate PDF capture without affecting the main UI. Cover the file naming convention, path structure, and metadata handling. Include examples of template rendering, canvas configuration options, and PDF output customization. Address performance considerations, memory management during large image processing, and fallback mechanisms for generation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Toggle Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the ThemeToggle component implementation. Explain the component's role in user interface theme switching, button styling with Tailwind CSS, and icon representation for light/dark modes. Document the integration with ThemeContext through the useTheme hook, click event handling, and theme state updates. Cover accessibility features including ARIA labels, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Include examples of component usage, styling customization, and integration with the overall theme system. Address responsive design considerations and mobile touch interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Automation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed email automation system documentation for MedVita's prescription delivery workflow. Document the Supabase edge function implementation for automated email generation and delivery. Explain the email template system, dynamic content injection, and PDF attachment handling. Cover the integration with Resend API for transactional email delivery, including authentication, rate limiting, and delivery tracking. Detail the prescription-to-email conversion process, including document generation, storage integration, and secure sharing mechanisms. Explain the function trigger conditions, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Include configuration examples for email templates, sender domains, and recipient management. Address security considerations for email automation, including data privacy and access controls. Provide monitoring and logging strategies for email delivery performance and troubleshooting guides for common delivery issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Edge Functions API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Supabase edge functions in MedVita. Document the send-prescription-email function including function parameters, input validation, and response formats. Explain the serverless function architecture, deployment process, and execution environment. Detail the email sending workflow including template rendering, attachment handling, and delivery confirmation. Document function triggers, timing, and integration with the main application. Include error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and logging approaches. Provide examples of function invocation, parameter passing, and result processing. Address security considerations, rate limiting, and performance optimization. Document integration patterns with the prescription creation workflow and notification system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Button Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Button component implementation. Document all available variants (primary, secondary, blue, danger, ghost, icon), size options (sm, md, lg, icon), and state management (loading, disabled). Explain the component's props interface, default values, and prop combinations. Include comprehensive usage examples showing different button types in various contexts. Document the animation system including hover effects, loading states, and transitions. Explain accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support. Provide guidelines for button placement, labeling best practices, and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for integration testing of MedVita's full-stack components. Document testing approaches for Supabase database operations, authentication flows, and real-time data synchronization. Explain testing strategies for context providers, custom hooks, and component integration patterns. Cover testing of API endpoints, database queries, and authentication middleware. Include examples of testing Supabase client operations, real-time subscriptions, and data consistency. Document testing of multi-component workflows, cross-module interactions, and external service integrations. Address testing of critical healthcare workflows, error handling, and edge cases. Provide guidance on test environment setup, database seeding, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive cloud deployment documentation for MedVita's multi-cloud architecture. Detail the Supabase deployment process including database provisioning, edge function deployment, and real-time subscription setup. Document the Vercel deployment configuration including environment variables, custom domains, and redirect rules. Explain the edge function deployment workflow for email automation and serverless processing. Cover environment variable management across different deployment stages, SSL certificate configuration, and CDN optimization. Include deployment verification procedures, health checks, and rollback mechanisms. Address cost optimization strategies, resource monitoring, and scaling considerations for production workloads.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vercel Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Vercel deployment documentation for the React frontend application. Detail the Vercel project configuration including build settings, environment variables, and custom domain setup. Document the deployment process using Vercel CLI and GitHub integration for automatic deployments. Explain environment variable management including .env.local configuration, Vercel project settings, and secret management. Cover build configuration optimization, static asset optimization, and CDN distribution. Detail custom domain configuration, SSL certificate management, and redirect rules defined in vercel.json. Include deployment verification procedures, preview deployment workflows, and production deployment triggers. Address Vercel Analytics integration, performance monitoring, and error tracking. Document rollback procedures, deployment logs analysis, and troubleshooting common deployment issues. Cover cost optimization strategies and resource monitoring for production traffic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for MedVita's system design. Describe the high-level architecture combining React frontend with Supabase backend, including the separation of concerns between client-side rendering and server-side database operations. Document the authentication flow using Supabase Auth, the role-based access control system, and real-time data synchronization through Supabase Realtime. Explain the edge functions architecture for email automation and external API integrations. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flows between frontend, backend, and external services. Address scalability considerations, security patterns, and deployment topology. Provide architectural diagrams showing the relationship between components and integration points.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive integration patterns documentation for MedVita's external service connectivity. Document the Google Calendar API integration pattern through googleCalendar.js, including OAuth authentication, event creation, and synchronization strategies. Explain the email automation workflow using Supabase edge functions, detailing the integration between frontend triggers and serverless email delivery. Document the Gemini AI API integration for chatbot functionality, including API key management, conversation handling, and fallback mechanisms. Detail the Supabase client integration patterns for database operations, authentication flows, and real-time subscriptions. Explain error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and circuit breaker patterns for external service failures. Include security considerations for API key management, rate limiting compliance, and data privacy protection. Address monitoring and logging patterns for external integrations, performance optimization techniques, and troubleshooting common integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Protected Routes & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for protected routes and access control implementation. Document the ProtectedRoute component architecture and how it enforces role-based access to different dashboard pages. Explain the integration between ProtectedRoute and AuthContext for real-time authentication state checking. Detail the route protection logic, redirect mechanisms for unauthorized users, and dynamic menu generation based on user roles. Include examples of protected route configuration, role-specific navigation, and conditional component rendering. Address access control patterns, route guards implementation, and security considerations for preventing direct URL access to protected resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Schemas & Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive table schema documentation for all MedVita database tables. Detail each table structure including profiles, patients, doctor_availability, appointments, and prescriptions with field definitions, data types, constraints, and default values. Document primary keys, foreign keys, unique constraints, and check constraints. Explain the purpose and business rules behind each field including generated identifiers, timestamps, and validation rules. Include table creation scripts with all constraints and indexes. Document the UUID extension usage and generated ID patterns. Cover migration scripts for schema evolution and backward compatibility. Provide examples of table relationships and constraint violations with their resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed routing system documentation for MedVita's React Router implementation. Document the routing configuration in App.jsx, including route definitions for different user roles and protected routes. Explain the ProtectedRoute component implementation for role-based navigation control. Detail the navigation patterns, route parameters handling, and programmatic navigation techniques. Include examples of dynamic routing, nested routes, and route guards. Address client-side routing best practices, navigation state management, and integration with authentication flows. Document the relationship between routing and component rendering, including lazy loading strategies for route components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Receptionist Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Receptionist Dashboard functionality in MedVita. Detail the front desk operations interface including patient check-in/out processes, appointment coordination, and administrative task management. Document the patient registration workflow, queue management for walk-in patients, and integration with the doctor dashboard for real-time updates. Explain the receptionist-specific navigation patterns, administrative controls, and staff coordination features. Cover the integration with the main dashboard system and how receptionists facilitate the overall clinic workflow. Include the receptionist's role in managing patient flow, coordinating with doctors, and maintaining clinic operations. Document the interface design optimized for front desk efficiency, quick access to frequently used functions, and integration with the broader healthcare management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Queue Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the patient queue management system in MedVita's reception workflow. Detail the front desk operations including patient check-in procedures, queue positioning, status tracking, and real-time queue updates. Document the integration with appointment scheduling, walk-in patient handling, and priority queuing systems. Explain the visual queue displays, patient notification systems, and staff assignment workflows. Cover the queue analytics, wait time tracking, and capacity management features. Include examples of queue management scenarios, staff-patient interaction workflows, and integration with billing systems. Address queue optimization strategies, overflow handling, and performance monitoring for busy healthcare environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Calendar Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Google Calendar integration system. Document the OAuth authentication flow, event creation and synchronization processes, and conflict resolution strategies. Explain the calendar event mapping between MedVita appointments and Google Calendar entries, including event metadata preservation and update propagation. Cover the manual sync fallback mechanisms, calendar link generation for direct user access, and error handling for sync failures. Detail the setup requirements, API configuration, and security considerations for calendar access. Include examples of successful sync scenarios, common sync issues, and troubleshooting procedures. Address the bidirectional synchronization implications and data consistency measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Handling Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for real-time event handling patterns used throughout MedVita. Explain the event-driven architecture for handling INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations on patient records, appointment status changes, and queue modifications. Document the payload processing pipeline, event type discrimination, and state update strategies. Cover the use of stable refs for callback closures, event batching for performance optimization, and error propagation in realtime handlers. Include examples of custom event processing, event filtering logic, and integration with React state management. Address event ordering guarantees, conflict resolution, and debugging techniques for complex event sequences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Storage Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the cloud storage integration with Supabase Storage. Document the file upload process including MIME type specification, cache control headers, and content security policies. Explain the file path structure organization by doctor ID and timestamp-based naming conventions. Cover the public URL generation process and access control mechanisms. Detail the storage bucket configuration, file size limitations, and security considerations. Include examples of upload error handling, retry mechanisms, and file cleanup procedures. Address storage optimization strategies, backup considerations, and compliance requirements for medical document retention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings Modal & User Preferences
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SettingsModal component and user preference management system. Document the modal interface design, preference categories (theme, density, app style), and form controls for user customization. Explain the integration with ThemeContext for preference persistence, state synchronization, and real-time preview capabilities. Cover the settings form implementation including input validation, default value handling, and preference reset functionality. Detail the modal's accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and focus management. Include examples of preference updates, localStorage integration, and customization workflows. Address responsive design considerations and mobile-first approach to settings accessibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Storage Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create cloud storage integration documentation for MedVita's document management system. Detail the integration with Supabase Storage for secure file handling, including prescription document storage, retrieval, and access control. Document the file upload processes, metadata management, and security policies. Explain the relationship between cloud storage and email automation, including document attachment generation and secure sharing mechanisms. Cover storage bucket configuration, access permissions, and data lifecycle management. Include examples of document types stored, size limitations, and performance considerations. Address security measures for document access, encryption at rest and in transit, and audit logging. Provide troubleshooting guides for upload failures, access denied errors, and storage quota management. Document backup and recovery procedures for critical medical documents.

wiki_catalogs.name: Realtime API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Realtime API documentation for MedVita's live data synchronization. Document Supabase Realtime channels, subscription management, and event handling patterns. Explain the live queue management system, real-time patient updates, and cross-module synchronization. Detail channel creation, subscription lifecycle, and event filtering mechanisms. Document event types including INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations and their payloads. Include practical examples of realtime subscriptions for dashboard components, queue management, and notification systems. Address connection management, reconnection strategies, and error recovery. Document performance considerations, bandwidth optimization, and scalability patterns. Explain integration with React components using hooks and state management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Card Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Card component specification. Document the component's structure, styling options, and content organization patterns. Explain how cards handle different types of content including text, images, forms, and interactive elements. Detail the component's responsive behavior, spacing, and layout capabilities. Include examples of card usage in dashboard layouts, data presentation, and form containers. Document accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation, and focus management. Provide guidelines for card composition, content hierarchy, and visual grouping patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MedVita's testing configuration and setup. Document the Vitest configuration file, test environment setup, and testing utilities initialization. Explain the Jest DOM setup, test globals, and testing framework configuration. Cover test runner settings, coverage configuration, and reporting options. Include examples of configuring test environments, setting up mock modules, and defining test aliases. Document the testing script configuration, watch mode settings, and CI/CD integration. Address test file organization, naming conventions, and test discovery patterns. Provide guidance on extending test configuration for new testing scenarios and maintaining configuration consistency across development teams.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed CI/CD pipeline documentation for MedVita's automated deployment workflow. Document the continuous integration setup including automated testing, code quality checks, and security scanning. Explain the continuous deployment pipeline configuration for staging and production environments. Detail the automated testing integration including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing. Cover the deployment automation scripts, environment promotion strategies, and rollback procedures. Include monitoring and alerting configuration for pipeline failures, deployment health checks, and performance metrics collection. Address pipeline optimization, parallel execution strategies, and artifact management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed environment management documentation covering multi-environment configuration across development, staging, and production environments. Document environment variable structure for both frontend (.env.local, .env.example) and backend (Supabase config) configurations. Explain environment-specific database connections, API endpoints, and service credentials. Detail the separation of sensitive data from code, secure credential storage, and environment variable encryption. Cover environment switching procedures, local development setup, and CI/CD pipeline environment configuration. Include environment validation procedures, configuration testing, and environment-specific feature flags. Document secrets management using platform-specific tools, rotation procedures, and access control. Address environment drift detection, configuration auditing, and compliance requirements for healthcare data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for MedVita. Detail the multi-role authentication system supporting Doctor, Patient, and Receptionist roles. Document the authentication flow from login to session management, including JWT token handling and profile creation. Explain the ProtectedRoute implementation for role-based access control and how it integrates with Supabase RLS policies. Cover session persistence, automatic logout mechanisms, and security considerations. Include practical examples of role checking in components, protected route configuration, and user permission management. Address common authentication scenarios, error handling, and troubleshooting authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for session management and security implementation in MedVita. Detail the JWT token handling, session persistence across browser refreshes, and automatic session restoration. Explain the Supabase auth state change listeners and how they maintain real-time authentication state synchronization. Document security measures including automatic logout on session expiration, concurrent session handling, and secure token storage. Cover session cleanup procedures, logout mechanisms, and security best practices for protecting user sessions. Include troubleshooting guides for common session-related issues, debugging authentication state problems, and implementing additional security layers like two-factor authentication if needed.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns & Queries
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MedVita's data access patterns and common query strategies. Document frequently used query patterns for each major feature including patient lookup, appointment scheduling, prescription retrieval, and dashboard reporting. Explain optimized query approaches for role-based data filtering, real-time data access, and cross-table joins. Include examples of complex queries for dashboard analytics, patient history tracking, and appointment management. Document query performance considerations, indexing strategies, and database optimization techniques. Cover pagination patterns, search functionality, and data filtering approaches. Provide troubleshooting guidance for slow queries and database performance issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theming & Styling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive theming system documentation for MedVita's Tailwind CSS-based styling approach. Document the ThemeContext implementation for managing light/dark mode states and theme preferences. Explain the ThemeToggle component functionality and user preference persistence. Detail the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS utility classes, custom CSS variables, and component-specific styling patterns. Include examples of theme-aware components, responsive design implementation, and accessibility considerations in the theming system. Address theme customization options, CSS-in-JS alternatives, and performance optimization for styling. Document the relationship between theming and component design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Navigation & Layout
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dashboard navigation system and layout architecture in MedVita. Document the responsive sidebar navigation with role-based menu items, breadcrumb navigation, and mobile-responsive design patterns. Explain the header components including user profile dropdown, notification system, and quick access controls. Detail the layout structure using CSS Grid and Flexbox for optimal dashboard arrangement across different screen sizes. Cover the navigation guards and route protection mechanisms for role-based access control. Document the layout customization options, theme integration, and accessibility features. Include the integration between navigation components and the overall dashboard routing system. Address the responsive design breakpoints, mobile navigation patterns, and touch-friendly interface elements. Explain the layout consistency across different dashboard roles and how the navigation adapts to various screen resolutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search & Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for patient search and filtering capabilities in MedVita. Document the multi-criteria search functionality including name-based searches, patient ID lookup, and vitals-based filtering. Explain the real-time search debouncing mechanism, search result ranking, and filter combination logic. Cover the date range filters (today, week, month, all), status-based filtering, and advanced search operators. Detail the mobile-responsive search interface, keyboard shortcuts, and accessibility features. Include examples of complex search queries, filter combinations, and performance optimization techniques. Address search indexing strategies, result pagination, and search analytics for improving user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Tracking & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the appointment status tracking and management system. Document the status lifecycle (scheduled, confirmed, completed, cancelled), status transition workflows, and user role-based status modifications. Explain the visual indicators for different statuses in calendar and list views, status filtering capabilities, and status-based notifications. Cover the administrative controls for status updates, rescheduling procedures, and cancellation policies. Detail the integration with notification systems and calendar sync for status changes. Include examples of common status workflows, approval processes, and status change audit trails. Address the impact of status changes on capacity management and scheduling conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for real-time communication performance optimization in MedVita. Detail connection pooling strategies, subscription lifecycle management, and memory leak prevention techniques. Explain event throttling, data transformation optimization, and efficient state updates for high-frequency realtime data. Document network resilience patterns, automatic reconnection logic, and fallback mechanisms for unreliable connections. Include metrics collection for realtime performance monitoring, connection health checks, and load balancing considerations. Address scaling challenges for concurrent users, database query optimization for realtime filters, and client-side caching strategies for reduced bandwidth usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Delivery System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the email delivery system powered by Supabase Edge Functions. Document the edge function architecture, including TypeScript implementation, dependency management, and environment variable configuration. Explain the email template system, recipient handling, and delivery confirmation mechanisms. Cover the integration with external email services, error handling strategies, and retry logic for failed deliveries. Detail the function invocation process from the frontend, parameter passing, and response handling. Include examples of email content customization, template variables, and delivery tracking. Address security considerations, rate limiting, and compliance requirements for medical information transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Third-Party API Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive third-party API authentication documentation for MedVita's external service integrations. Detail the OAuth 2.0 implementation for Google Calendar, including client credentials management, authorization flows, and token refresh mechanisms. Document the authentication state management, including session persistence, token validation, and logout procedures. Explain the security considerations for API key storage, token encryption, and credential rotation. Cover the implementation of different authentication methods, including OAuth 2.0, API keys, and JWT tokens. Include environment variable configuration, secret management, and development vs production authentication differences. Address common authentication issues such as expired tokens, invalid scopes, and permission denials. Provide debugging tools and techniques for authentication troubleshooting, including token inspection and network request analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication API documentation for MedVita's user authentication and authorization system. Document Supabase Auth integration including login/logout operations, session management, and token handling. Explain the multi-role authentication system supporting Doctor, Patient, and Receptionist roles. Detail user registration workflows, password reset procedures, and account verification processes. Document JWT token structure, expiration handling, and refresh mechanisms. Include role-based access control implementation, permission checking, and route protection patterns. Provide examples of authentication state management, protected route implementation, and user profile operations. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, secure cookies, and session invalidation. Document integration with Supabase Row Level Security policies and database access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Input component implementation. Document input types, validation states, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Explain the component's integration with form libraries, controlled/uncontrolled usage patterns, and event handling. Detail styling options, placeholder behavior, and focus states. Include examples of different input configurations including text inputs, password fields, search bars, and specialized medical input formats. Document accessibility features including ARIA labels, validation announcements, and keyboard navigation. Provide guidelines for input validation, error messaging, and user experience optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MedVita's quality assurance processes and metrics. Document the testing report analysis, code quality metrics, and performance testing results. Explain the continuous integration testing pipeline, automated quality checks, and regression testing strategies. Cover test coverage analysis, code quality reports, and build verification processes. Include examples of interpreting test reports, identifying quality issues, and tracking improvement metrics. Document the relationship between testing, linting, and build processes. Address quality gates, automated testing workflows, and quality assurance best practices for healthcare applications. Provide guidance on maintaining code quality standards and establishing quality metrics for medical software development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create production monitoring documentation for MedVita's operational excellence framework. Document application performance monitoring including response times, error rates, and user experience metrics. Detail infrastructure monitoring covering database performance, edge function execution, and cloud resource utilization. Explain logging strategies, log aggregation, and alerting configuration for critical system events. Cover health check endpoints, uptime monitoring, and incident response procedures. Include capacity planning guidelines, performance optimization recommendations, and scalability monitoring. Address security monitoring, audit logging, and compliance reporting requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment monitoring documentation covering health checks, performance metrics, and troubleshooting procedures. Document application health monitoring including database connectivity checks, edge function execution monitoring, and frontend performance metrics. Detail logging strategies for Supabase edge functions, Vercel deployment logs, and application error tracking. Explain alerting mechanisms for deployment failures, performance degradation, and security incidents. Cover monitoring dashboard setup using platform-specific tools, custom metric collection, and automated health checks. Include troubleshooting procedures for common deployment issues, rollback mechanisms, and incident response workflows. Document performance optimization monitoring, resource utilization tracking, and capacity planning indicators. Address compliance monitoring for healthcare applications, audit logging, and data protection metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for MedVita's database schema. Detail all entities including profiles, patients, appointments, prescriptions, and doctor_availability with their relationships and constraints. Document primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and database policies. Explain the Row Level Security implementation for data isolation between user roles. Include entity relationship diagrams and table schemas with field definitions and data types. Document data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity. Cover data access patterns, query optimization strategies, and performance considerations. Address data lifecycle management, backup strategies, and schema migration approaches. Include practical examples of common queries and data operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Integration & Policies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MedVita's storage integration with Supabase Storage. Detail the storage bucket configuration, file upload policies, and access control mechanisms for prescription documents and medical files. Document the storage.buckets table setup, object policies for authenticated access, and file management workflows. Explain the relationship between prescriptions table and stored files, including file URL generation and retrieval. Cover storage security policies, public vs private file access, and file lifecycle management. Include examples of file upload processes, URL generation, and document retrieval patterns. Address storage optimization, file naming conventions, and backup considerations for medical documents.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration & Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed build configuration documentation for MedVita's Vite-based development environment. Document the Vite configuration in vite.config.js, including build optimization settings, asset handling, and development server configuration. Explain the package.json scripts for development, building, and deployment workflows. Detail the ESLint configuration for code quality enforcement and development standards. Include environment variable management, build optimization techniques, and bundle analysis strategies. Address development server features, hot module replacement, and debugging configurations. Document the integration between Vite, React, and Supabase client libraries for optimal development experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for patient management workflow integration across MedVita's healthcare ecosystem. Detail the integration points with appointment scheduling, prescription creation, billing systems, and laboratory results. Document the patient journey mapping from registration through treatment completion, including handoff procedures between different care providers. Explain the data synchronization mechanisms, workflow automation triggers, and cross-system notifications. Cover the integration with external healthcare systems, insurance verification processes, and referral management. Address workflow optimization opportunities, bottleneck identification, and performance monitoring for complex care pathways.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prescription Viewer and History
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the prescription viewer and history tracking system. Document the patient-facing interface for viewing prescription history, including filtering capabilities, search functionality, and pagination controls. Explain the data retrieval patterns from Supabase, including role-based access restrictions and patient record associations. Cover the prescription display formatting, including diagnosis and treatment sections, doctor information, and timestamp details. Detail the integration with cloud storage for PDF access, download functionality, and print capabilities. Include examples of prescription status tracking, refill request mechanisms, and integration with pharmacy systems. Address security measures for protecting patient medical information and maintaining audit trails.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Badge component specification. Document badge variants, color schemes, and semantic meanings for different statuses and categories. Include examples of badge usage in patient records, appointment status, system alerts, and filtering interfaces. Document accessibility considerations, screen reader compatibility, and dynamic content updates. Provide guidelines for badge composition, color psychology, and visual hierarchy in healthcare contexts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for MedVita's React application. Describe the component hierarchy, routing configuration using React Router, and state management patterns with React Context API. Document the layout system, theme provider implementation, and component composition strategies. Explain the build configuration with Vite, asset management, and development workflow. Include code organization patterns, folder structure rationale, and development best practices. Address performance optimization techniques, lazy loading strategies, and bundle analysis. Provide guidance for extending the frontend architecture and adding new features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Customization & Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for customizing and extending MedVita's UI component library. wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive dashboard system documentation for MedVita's role-based dashboards. Document the Patient Dashboard features covering appointment booking, medical history access, and health statistics. Explain the Receptionist Dashboard for front desk operations, patient check-in, and administrative tasks. Include navigation structure, dashboard layouts, and real-time data synchronization. Cover the integration between dashboards and backend services, data visualization components, and user interaction patterns. Address dashboard customization options, performance considerations for real-time updates, and accessibility features. Provide examples of dashboard extensions and custom widget development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed patient management documentation for MedVita's healthcare workflow system. Document patient registration processes, profile management, and medical history tracking. Explain the queue management system for front desk operations, patient check-in procedures, and staff-patient interactions. Cover patient search and filtering capabilities, profile updates, and emergency contact management. Detail the integration with appointment scheduling, prescription creation, and billing systems. Include workflow patterns for patient onboarding, status tracking, and discharge procedures. Address data privacy considerations, patient consent management, and audit trails. wiki_catalogs.name: Appointment Scheduling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive appointment scheduling documentation for MedVita's booking system. Detail the patient-facing appointment booking interface, doctor availability management, and real-time slot coordination. Document the calendar integration with Google Calendar, including event synchronization and conflict resolution. Explain the availability configuration system for doctors, including working hours, break scheduling, and special appointment types. Cover appointment status tracking, rescheduling procedures, and cancellation policies. Include integration with patient management, doctor scheduling, and notification systems. Address capacity management, waitlist functionality, and resource allocation. Provide examples of custom scheduling rules, bulk availability updates, and integration with external calendar systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prescription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed prescription management documentation for MedVita's digital prescription system. Document the prescription creation interface, including medication selection, dosage instructions, and patient information integration. Cover prescription viewing and history tracking for patients, including refill requests and medication adherence monitoring. Include integration with pharmacy systems, insurance verification, and electronic prescribing networks. Address prescription security, tamper-proof documentation, and audit trail requirements. Provide examples of prescription templates, custom medication databases, and integration with medical reference systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive real-time communication documentation for MedVita's live data synchronization system. Detail the Supabase Realtime implementation for live queue updates, appointment status changes, and patient monitoring. Explain the subscription management, event handling, and data consistency patterns. Document the real-time dashboard updates, instant notifications, and cross-module synchronization. Cover performance optimization for real-time data streams, connection management, and fallback mechanisms. Include integration with push notifications, email alerts, and SMS messaging systems. Address scalability considerations for concurrent users, data broadcasting strategies, and network resilience. Provide examples of custom real-time events, data transformation pipelines, and monitoring real-time system performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Chatbot Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed AI chatbot integration documentation for MedVita's conversational assistance system. Document the Gemini AI API integration, conversation management, and natural language processing capabilities. Explain the chatbot architecture, including intent recognition, context management, and response generation. Detail the fallback mechanisms for unavailable AI responses, human agent handoff procedures, and conversation logging. Cover the integration with patient information, appointment scheduling, and common healthcare inquiries. Include customization options for chatbot personality, response templates, and domain-specific knowledge bases. Address privacy considerations for health information, compliance requirements, and conversation security. Provide examples of chatbot training data, conversation flows, and integration with external healthcare resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive UI components library documentation for MedVita's design system. Detail all reusable components including Button, Card, Input, Badge, and other UI primitives. Document component props, attributes, events, and customization options. Explain the design system principles, color schemes, typography, and spacing conventions. Include usage examples with code snippets, live demonstrations, and accessibility compliance. Cover component states, animations, transitions, and responsive behavior. Document theming support, style customization, and integration patterns with other UI elements. Address cross-browser compatibility, performance optimization, and component composition strategies. Provide guidelines for extending the component library and creating new custom components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme & Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed theme and customization documentation for MedVita's visual design system. Document the ThemeProvider implementation, dark/light mode switching, and theme persistence across sessions. Explain the customization options including color schemes, typography adjustments, and layout modifications. Detail the SettingsModal functionality for user preferences, interface density controls, and accessibility features. Cover the integration between theme context, component styling, and user preference management. Include examples of custom theme creation, brand customization, and responsive design adaptations. Address accessibility compliance, contrast ratios, and inclusive design practices. Provide guidance for extending the theming system and implementing custom design tokens.

wiki_catalogs.name: External Integrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive external integrations documentation for MedVita's third-party service connectivity. Detail the Google Calendar API integration for appointment synchronization, event creation, and calendar management. Document the email automation system using Supabase edge functions, including template management and delivery tracking. Explain the integration setup process, authentication mechanisms, and error handling strategies. Cover the Resend API integration for transactional emails, cloud storage integration for document management, and potential future integrations. Include configuration examples, webhook handling, and data synchronization patterns. Address security considerations for external API access, rate limiting, and failure recovery. Provide troubleshooting guides for common integration issues and monitoring integration health.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for MedVita's public interfaces and endpoints. Document the Supabase REST API integration, including authentication methods, CRUD operations, and query patterns. Detail the edge functions API for email automation, including function parameters, response formats, and error handling. Explain the Supabase Realtime API for real-time data synchronization, including channel management and event subscriptions. Cover the authentication endpoints, session management, and user profile APIs. Include request/response schemas, parameter validation, and error codes. Document rate limiting, pagination strategies, and API versioning. Provide practical examples of API usage, client implementation guidelines, and integration patterns. Address security considerations, CORS configuration, and API monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for MedVita's quality assurance approach. Detail the unit testing framework using Vitest, component testing patterns, and test configuration. Document the testing setup, mock strategies, and test environment configuration. Explain the testing approach for React components, custom hooks, context providers, and API integrations. Cover integration testing for Supabase operations, authentication flows, and real-time data synchronization. Include testing best practices, test coverage requirements, and continuous integration patterns. Address testing of critical healthcare workflows, error scenarios, and edge cases. Provide examples of test implementation, debugging test failures, and maintaining test suites. Document the testing report analysis and quality metrics tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for MedVita's production deployment pipeline. Detail the build configuration using Vite, asset optimization, and deployment preparation. Document the Supabase deployment process, including database migrations, edge function deployment, and environment configuration. Explain the Vercel deployment setup, including environment variables, custom domains, and redirect rules. Cover the CI/CD pipeline configuration, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Include production environment setup, monitoring configuration, and rollback procedures. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, monitoring deployment health, and scaling considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for MedVita's coding standards and development practices. Detail the code formatting standards using ESLint, component naming conventions, and file organization patterns. Document the development workflow including branch strategies, pull request guidelines, and code review processes. Explain the testing requirements, commit message conventions, and documentation standards. Cover the dependency management, version control practices, and security scanning procedures. Include best practices for React development, performance optimization, and accessibility compliance. Address debugging techniques, profiling tools, and development environment setup. wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for MedVita's common issues and solutions. Document frequent setup problems including environment configuration, dependency installation, and database connection issues. Address runtime errors, authentication failures, and real-time synchronization problems. Explain debugging techniques for frontend components, backend services, and integration issues. Include performance troubleshooting for slow loading times, memory leaks, and database query optimization.
